answer:
**The work is *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana* (by Mpu Dusun, in Kawi), Chapter 1.**[^1]

In this Buddhist didactic tale, the ascetic *rākṣasa* Kuñjarakarṇa—sent by the Buddha Vairocana on a hell-tour to witness sin's consequences—notices a "great copper cauldron with many spouts" in the courtyard of Yama's radiant palace while visiting the Lord of Death (Yama).[^2] Puzzled that attendants are scrubbing it clean despite its being empty and upturned, he asks why.[^3]

> \"Lord Yama, may your glory be eternal.  
> Your compassion for this Kuñjara is fruitful.  
> But what is that cauldron that is being washed?  
> What is its purpose, now that it is clean?\"[^4]  
>   
> \"I am puzzled by it, for it is to be filled,  
> yet it is turned upside down, with nothing inside.  
> What is the reason for it being so empty?  
> This is my question; please tell me.\"[^5]

Yama explains it is for a great sinner (the *gandharva*-king Pūrṇavijaya) due to arrive in seven nights, whose term there will be a hundred thousand years (followed by impalement on an iron/weapon-tree by sword-birds and suspension head-down from a bamboo pole over fire on the iron plain).[^6]

> \"There is a great sinner who is to be cast in.  
> His time in torment will be long.  
> For a hundred thousand years is his term;  
> when it is full, he will be in the cauldron of misery.\"[^7]  
>   
> \"But as for his arrival in Yama's realm,  
> in seven nights he is expected to come.  
> That is the reason the cauldron is being washed,  
> as the place of his torment when he arrives.\"[^8]

[^1]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/).

[^2]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-693983](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-693983).

[^3]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-c32761](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c32761) to [p-2efb11](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-2efb11).

[^4]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-c32761](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c32761).

[^5]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-2efb11](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-2efb11).

[^6]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-f2b796](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f2b796) to [p-c757ea](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c757ea).

[^7]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-f2b796](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f2b796).

[^8]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [p-c757ea](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-c757ea).
